title: Decomp standard: Recover natural loops before preserving repeated generated blocks
text:
Recover natural loops before preserving repeated generated blocks
Repeated blocks that vary by an index, pointer, flag slot, or stride are often original loops. Try natural for/while/do-while forms, including exact int counters when MWCC loop shape matters, before keeping copied generated blocks.
Use for, while, or do while when repeated source differs only by index, pointer, counter, or stride. Keep the original base pointer available when later code still needs it. Record verifier evidence if cleaner loop forms fail and an unrolled shape remains.
Leave repeated generated-looking blocks only because they currently match. Mutate the only base pointer copy when later codegen still needs the base address. Treat unrolled code as finished source without checking natural loop forms.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#recover-natural-loops docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

record.title: Recover natural loops before preserving repeated generated blocks
record.summary: Repeated blocks that vary by an index, pointer, flag slot, or stride are often original loops. Try natural for/while/do-while forms, including exact int counters when MWCC loop shape matters, before keeping copied generated blocks.
do: Use for, while, or do while when repeated source differs only by index, pointer, counter, or stride.
Keep the original base pointer available when later code still needs it.
Record verifier evidence if cleaner loop forms fail and an unrolled shape remains.
do_not: Leave repeated generated-looking blocks only because they currently match.
Mutate the only base pointer copy when later codegen still needs the base address.
Treat unrolled code as finished source without checking natural loop forms.

title: Decomp standard: Infer authored source style from local analogs
text:
Infer authored source style from local analogs
Matching should reconstruct likely original authored source, not arbitrary C that moves a score. Use matched siblings, nearby files, headers, macros, naming habits, control-flow idioms, and historical PR evidence to infer the original developers' local standards before preserving generated, permuter-shaped, or data-driven source.
Treat matched local source and headers as evidence for how original developers in the subsystem wrote loops, helpers, names, declarations, macros, and access patterns. Prefer a source hypothesis that fits local authored style and verifies with objdiff over one that only chases data parity or decompiler output. Record negative evidence when local analogs contradict a generated or external suggestion.
Preserve generated, permuter-shaped, or data-driven source when nearby human-written code shows a cleaner local convention. Let external mirrors, decompiler output, AI output, or data-section parity outrank matched local source and headers. Call a fake or unusual shape reviewable merely because it improves a fuzzy score.
user-provided agent prompt direction: infer original developer standards from neighboring source src/agents/worker/templates/system.md#source_standardization_rules

title: Decomp standard: Prefer typed fields, union arms, and accessors over pointer math
text:
Prefer typed fields, union arms, and accessors over pointer math
When a type can describe an access, prefer real fields, correct FighterVars/GroundVars/ItemVars union arms, accessors, or temporary internal structs before M2C_FIELD and raw pointer arithmetic.
Replace raw offsets with real fields when the type is known. Use correct fighter, ground, and item union arms instead of borrowing unrelated fields. Treat M2C_FIELD as bridge code while recovering a type or field.
Keep ((u8*) obj) + offset when a field, union arm, helper, or temporary struct can express the access. Add new M2C_FIELD uses when the real type can reasonably be recovered. Use unrelated union arms just because generated output guessed them.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#prefer-typed-fields-over-pointer-math docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

do: Replace raw offsets with real fields when the type is known.
Use correct fighter, ground, and item union arms instead of borrowing unrelated fields.
Treat M2C_FIELD as bridge code while recovering a type or field.
do_not: Keep ((u8*) obj) + offset when a field, union arm, helper, or temporary struct can express the access.
Add new M2C_FIELD uses when the real type can reasonably be recovered.
Use unrelated union arms just because generated output guessed them.

title: Decomp standard: Recognize header inlines instead of keeping expanded assert code
text:
Recognize header inlines instead of keeping expanded assert code
Expanded header asserts, especially from jobj.h, should be mapped back to the existing inline/helper when line numbers and local evidence identify the source-level operation.
Use assert file and line numbers to find the owning header inline. Prefer existing HSD_JObjSet*, HSD_JObjGet*, and local inline forms when they represent the operation. Use GET_JOBJ only when local evidence supports that access shape.
Keep expanded __assert("jobj.h", line, ...) when the header inline is identifiable. Treat GET_JOBJ as unconditional cleanup. Redefine or manually expand header helpers to force a match without objdiff evidence.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#recognize-header-inlines docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

do: Use assert file and line numbers to find the owning header inline.
Prefer existing HSD_JObjSet*, HSD_JObjGet*, and local inline forms when they represent the operation.
Use GET_JOBJ only when local evidence supports that access shape.
do_not: Keep expanded __assert("jobj.h", line, ...) when the header inline is identifiable.
Treat GET_JOBJ as unconditional cleanup.
Redefine or manually expand header helpers to force a match without objdiff evidence.

title: Decomp standard: Use project assert and report macros when they represent the source
text:
Use project assert and report macros when they represent the source
Raw assert/report expansions should usually become HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, or OSPanic forms when the file, line, message, and side effects match local project idioms.
Use HSD_ASSERT for plain assertions. Use HSD_ASSERTMSG or HSD_ASSERTREPORT when message or report side effects are real. Preserve real OSReport and OSPanic behavior while cleaning up generated assert blocks.
Leave direct __assert blocks when an existing macro is the source-level form. Invent fake assert strings, report globals, or dummy storage to force a match. Drop a real report or panic side effect.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#use-project-assert-and-report-macros docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Use HSD_ASSERTMSG or HSD_ASSERTREPORT when message or report side effects are real.
Preserve real OSReport and OSPanic behavior while cleaning up generated assert blocks.
do_not: Leave direct __assert blocks when an existing macro is the source-level form.
Invent fake assert strings, report globals, or dummy storage to force a match.
Drop a real report or panic side effect.

title: Decomp standard: Keep literals inline unless data ownership evidence says otherwise
text:
Keep literals inline unless data ownership evidence says otherwise
Do not promote strings, floats, or constants into named storage just because generated output exposed an address. Check section ownership, symbol metadata, split boundaries, and local source patterns first.
Keep constants, strings, and float literals inline when no data ownership evidence exists. Verify .data, .rodata, .sdata, and .sdata2 ownership before naming data. Use named data when symbol metadata, section placement, or surrounding source supports it.
Create static literals or globals solely to force data order. Add fake data anchors, dummy statics, or data-order globals as normal style. Move literals across translation units without split evidence.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#keep-literals-inline-unless-data-evidence-says-otherwise docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Use named data when symbol metadata, section placement, or surrounding source supports it.
do_not: Create static literals or globals solely to force data order.
Add fake data anchors, dummy statics, or data-order globals as normal style.
Move literals across translation units without split evidence.

title: Decomp standard: Do not replace string literals with data symbols
text:
Do not replace string literals with data symbols
String literals used as asset names, resource labels, assert/report text, and table labels should stay inline for code-matching PRs. Replacing a literal with a generated or global data symbol is data work and a regression unless evidence and PR scope justify it.
Keep source string literals inline when they are ordinary asset names, table labels, assert/report text, or resource names. Treat string-literal-to-symbol changes as data ownership work requiring explicit evidence and PR scope. Prefer a code match that leaves the literal in place when the alternative only changes data-section relocation.
Replace a literal such as "MenMainBack_Top_joint" with a symbol such as mnNameNew_803EE38C to chase data parity. Convert strings into named globals because generated output exposed an address or another data section looks closer. Retain a string-symbol replacement that creates report noise unless the PR is explicitly about data ownership and evidence explains the tradeoff.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#do-not-replace-string-literals-with-data-symbols user-provided PR review comment: Replacing the string with the variable is a regression.

title: Decomp standard: Match text before chasing data sections
text:
Match text before chasing data sections
For matching PRs, text-section function progress is the primary objective. Data, literal, symbol, and split edits are high-risk secondary work: keep them only when required for the claimed code match, backed by section ownership or symbol metadata, or explicitly scoped as data cleanup.
Prioritize verified text-section function closure or matched-code progress over data-section parity. Keep data, literal, symbol, or split edits only when they are required for the code match, backed by section ownership or metadata, or explicitly scoped as data cleanup. Narrow or revert data edits that create noisy report regressions or false-positive section movement when the code match can land without them. Defer broad data-section matching until the translation unit's text section is complete unless a data owner issue blocks the code match.
Chase data-section parity as routine matching work before the translation unit's text section is complete. Add or move static data, literal externs, local assert overrides, fake helpers, fake anchors, or split churn solely to quiet a data diff. Mix a focused code match with unrelated .sdata, .sdata2, .rodata, or symbol cleanup. Retain data changes that create section-regression noise unless the PR is explicitly about data and the evidence explains the tradeoff.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#match-text-before-chasing-data knowledge/sources/code_context/past_prs/data/prs/pr-2510/postmortem.json#review_feedback knowledge/sources/code_context/past_prs/data/prs/pr-2510/postmortem.json#decomp_lessons

Narrow or revert data edits that create noisy report regressions or false-positive section movement when the code match can land without them.
Defer broad data-section matching until the translation unit's text section is complete unless a data owner issue blocks the code match.
do_not: Chase data-section parity as routine matching work before the translation unit's text section is complete.
Add or move static data, literal externs, local assert overrides, fake helpers, fake anchors, or split churn solely to quiet a data diff.
Mix a focused code match with unrelated .sdata, .sdata2, .rodata, or symbol cleanup.
Retain data changes that create section-regression noise unless the PR is explicitly about data and the evidence explains the tradeoff.

title: Decomp standard: Use canonical control flow and expression macros
text:
Use canonical control flow and expression macros
Generated branch ladders, gotos, repeated select-like assignments, and raw absolute/min/max expressions should be checked against normal source forms such as switch, ternary, ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP.
Try grouped switch forms for range dispatch or adjacent branches. Try ternaries for select-like assignments. Use project macros such as ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP when they express the source operation.
Preserve generated gotos or branch ladders before testing ordinary C forms. Replace readable control flow with fake code solely for one local sequence. Ignore common expression macros when assembly shape suggests them.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#use-canonical-control-flow-and-macros docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Try ternaries for select-like assignments.
Use project macros such as ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP when they express the source operation.
do_not: Preserve generated gotos or branch ladders before testing ordinary C forms.
Replace readable control flow with fake code solely for one local sequence.
Ignore common expression macros when assembly shape suggests them.

title: Decomp standard: Matching tactics require targeted evidence
text:
Matching tactics require targeted evidence
PAD_STACK, declaration order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy locals, volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs need explicit build/objdiff/regression evidence before they remain in reviewable source.
Keep tactic use narrow and record the verifier evidence that justified it. Check adjacent functions when a tactic can perturb codegen, data order, or local stack shape. Prefer cleanup once the underlying type, control flow, or data ownership is understood.
Use a tactic if it shifts nearby functions or hides a real type. Treat stack/register/local churn as permanent style without objdiff benefit. Keep broad tactics without a cleanup reason.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#require-evidence-for-matching-tactics docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

do: Keep tactic use narrow and record the verifier evidence that justified it.
Check adjacent functions when a tactic can perturb codegen, data order, or local stack shape.
Prefer cleanup once the underlying type, control flow, or data ownership is understood.
do_not: Use a tactic if it shifts nearby functions or hides a real type.
Treat stack/register/local churn as permanent style without objdiff benefit.
Keep broad tactics without a cleanup reason.

title: Decomp standard: Avoid new pragmas, register steering, and inline assembly for normal source
text:
Avoid new pragmas, register steering, and inline assembly for normal source
Pragmas, register keywords, and inline assembly are exceptions, not ordinary decomp style. Scope unavoidable exceptions tightly and prove they do not harm neighbors.
Scope unavoidable pragmas with tight push/pop blocks. Prove pragma or inline-assembly exceptions with adjacent-function evidence. Remove register and similar steering when it is not required.
Add #pragma global_optimizer off for normal source. Leave unbalanced or empty pragma blocks. Use inline assembly outside SDK-like code when reasonable C alternatives remain.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#avoid-new-pragmas-register-and-inline-assembly docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Prove pragma or inline-assembly exceptions with adjacent-function evidence.
Remove register and similar steering when it is not required.
do_not: Add #pragma global_optimizer off for normal source.
Leave unbalanced or empty pragma blocks.
Use inline assembly outside SDK-like code when reasonable C alternatives remain.

title: Decomp standard: Respect data sections and translation-unit split evidence
text:
Respect data sections and translation-unit split evidence
Section placement, symbol size/scope, local labels, assert filenames, string clusters, object names, and float groups are part of matching. Fix metadata instead of compensating with fake C storage.
Treat .data, .rodata, .sdata, .sdata2, .sbss, and split ownership as match evidence. Fix symbols.txt size, type, scope, or local labels when metadata is wrong. Use assert filenames, object names, strings, and float groups as TU split evidence.
Add dummy C storage to compensate for bad symbol metadata. Move data across translation units without split evidence. Treat section placement as cosmetic.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#respect-data-sections-and-tu-splits docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

record.title: Respect data sections and translation-unit split evidence
record.summary: Section placement, symbol size/scope, local labels, assert filenames, string clusters, object names, and float groups are part of matching. Fix metadata instead of compensating with fake C storage.
do: Treat .data, .rodata, .sdata, .sdata2, .sbss, and split ownership as match evidence.
Fix symbols.txt size, type, scope, or local labels when metadata is wrong.
Use assert filenames, object names, strings, and float groups as TU split evidence.
do_not: Add dummy C storage to compensate for bad symbol metadata.
Move data across translation units without split evidence.
Treat section placement as cosmetic.

title: Decomp standard: Use semantic names only when the role is evidenced
text:
Use semantic names only when the role is evidenced
Names should improve reviewability without inventing meaning. Use semantic names when supported, keep fn_/lbl_/address-style names when semantics are not proven, and clean temp_rXX names once a local role is clear.
Use semantic names when source, callsite, data, or PR evidence supports the role. Keep address-style names when meaning is not proven. Use short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj when roles are clear.
Invent semantic names from guesses. Rename address-style symbols before source meaning is supported. Leave temp_rXX-style names when a clear local role is known.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#be-conservative-with-names docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Keep address-style names when meaning is not proven.
Use short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj when roles are clear.
do_not: Invent semantic names from guesses.
Rename address-style symbols before source meaning is supported.
Leave temp_rXX-style names when a clear local role is known.

title: Decomp standard: Do not alias global renames with defines
text:
Do not alias global renames with defines
A global, extern, or data symbol should have one canonical name backed by source, symbol, map, or review evidence. Do not hide speculative or convenience renames behind identifier-to-identifier defines, and do not keep duplicate address-commented extern declarations for the same data address.
Preserve existing global, data, and extern names unless the rename is evidence-backed. Keep one canonical declaration for one known data address in the owning scope. Use a real scoped rename only when evidence supports it and update references directly.
Add #define old_name new_name or #define new_name old_name to hide a renamed variable. Keep two extern declarations with different names for the same address-commented data symbol. Rename address-style globals to semantic names because an external mirror, decompiler, or AI output guessed the role. Use a define as an alias to avoid updating call sites or to make a guessed semantic name appear accepted.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#do-not-alias-global-renames-with-defines user-provided PR review comment: Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.
evidence_ref: docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#do-not-alias-global-renames-with-defines;user-provided PR review comment: Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.

title: Decomp standard: Keep headers, prototypes, and includes truthful
text:
Keep headers, prototypes, and includes truthful
When a body proves a signature, update the owning header and remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS. Use established include style and run require-protos checks when prototype or include surfaces change.
Update prototypes when a function body proves the signature. Put declarations in the real owning header. Use established local include style and run python configure.py --require-protos when applicable.
Add fake source-local declarations when the real header should own them. Leave mismatched prototypes after recovering a function body. Hide missing prototypes behind local declarations.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#keep-headers-and-includes-truthful docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Put declarations in the real owning header.

do_not: Add fake source-local declarations when the real header should own them.
Leave mismatched prototypes after recovering a function body.
Hide missing prototypes behind local declarations.

title: Decomp standard: Claim matches only with build, objdiff, and regression evidence
text:
Claim matches only with build, objdiff, and regression evidence
Every claimed matched symbol, score candidate, or source cleanup must name the narrow build/objdiff/checkdiff evidence and any adjacent regression checks needed for the edit's blast radius.
Run narrow touched-object builds when possible. Run objdiff or checkdiff for claimed matches or score candidates. Inspect adjacent functions when changing pragmas, includes, data, literals, or TU ownership.
Claim a match without local build and objdiff/checkdiff evidence. Hide regressions behind a broad builds-locally statement. Mix tooling changes and decomp source changes when either can be reviewed separately.
docs/20-implementation/knowledge/20-melee-pr-review-qa-standards.md#verification-rules docs/20-implementation/knowledge/21-melee-pr-review-qa-coverage-audit.md#counted-standards

Run objdiff or checkdiff for claimed matches or score candidates.
Inspect adjacent functions when changing pragmas, includes, data, literals, or TU ownership.
do_not: Claim a match without local build and objdiff/checkdiff evidence.
Hide regressions behind a broad builds-locally statement.
Mix tooling changes and decomp source changes when either can be reviewed separately.
